title: Push Button Magic
subtitle: press, glow, learn!
overview: Students learn how push buttons work in real life by building a simple breadboard circuit using a battery, LED, resistor and push button. They understand how pressing a button completes a circuit and allows electricity to flow.

images._note: Add real classroom activity photos and breadboard connection pictures.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Close-up photo of push button switch
gallery.caption: Push button acts like a temporary ON switch

gallery._placeholder: ADD: LED glowing after button press
gallery.caption: LED glows only when the button is pressed
Students building push button circuit on breadboard
Doorbells use push buttons to complete circuits
Game controllers work using push buttons
Elevator buttons send signals when pressed

keyTopics: Circuit completes only when button is pressed
Resistor protects LED from too much current
Real-life buttons: Doorbell, Keyboard, Elevator, TV Remote

activity.title: Push Button LED Circuit
steps: Connect resistor to LED positive leg
Place push button across breadboard gap
Connect battery positive to push button
Connect LED negative to battery negative
Press the button to complete the circuit
Observe the LED glowing only while pressing the button

quiz.q: What happens when you press a push button in a circuit?
quiz.explanation: Pressing the button closes the circuit and allows electricity to flow.

quiz.q: Why does the LED glow when the button is pressed?
options: Because electricity flows through the circuit
quiz.explanation: The LED glows because current flows through it when the circuit is complete.

quiz.q: What is the role of the resistor in the circuit?
quiz.explanation: The resistor protects the LED from too much current.

quiz.q: A push button is an example of:
quiz.explanation: Push buttons act like temporary switches.

quiz.q: What type of circuit is formed when the button is NOT pressed?
quiz.explanation: Without pressing the button, the circuit path is broken.

quiz.q: Which real-life device uses push buttons?
quiz.explanation: All these devices use push buttons to send signals.

quiz.q: What does a battery provide in a circuit?
quiz.explanation: Battery provides electrical energy to the circuit.

quiz.q: What happens if there is no battery in the circuit?
quiz.explanation: Without battery there is no electrical energy.

quiz.q: What is a breadboard used for?
quiz.explanation: Breadboards help build and test circuits easily.

quiz.q: What does an LED convert electricity into?
quiz.explanation: LED converts electrical energy into light energy.

quiz.q: What is another name for a complete circuit?
quiz.explanation: A closed circuit allows electricity to flow.

quiz.q: Which leg of LED is usually longer?
quiz.explanation: The longer leg is usually the positive terminal.

quiz.q: Why is the push button called temporary switch?
options: Because it works only while pressing
quiz.explanation: The button works only while it is being pressed.

quiz.q: What happens when the button is released?
quiz.explanation: Releasing the button breaks the circuit again.

quiz.q: Which component glows in this activity?
quiz.explanation: The LED is the glowing component in the circuit.
